title: Onondaga Lake restoration is far from finished (Your Letters)
summary: Opinion letter criticizing County Executive McMahon's allocation of $2.5M in Honeywell remediation funds to the Inner Harbor aquarium instead of completing Onondaga Lake restoration. Letter highlights ongoing sewage management failures, worker safety issues at a $23M biosolids facility, and advocates for prioritizing lake cleanup and return of Maple Bay to the Onondaga Nation.

political_signal.description: Constituent opposition to county funding priorities; calls for reallocation of remediation funds from aquarium to lake restoration; advocates for tribal consultation and return of Maple Bay to Onondaga Nation.

processing_notes: Article is opinion/letters section; represents constituent viewpoint, not official announcement
Dollar amount '$2.5 million' relates to aquarium funding diversion; '$23 million' biosolids facility mentioned as context
References 2011/2016 county promises regarding Onondaga Lake remediation
June sewage discharge fine and recent worker safety issue at biosolids facility are cited as evidence of incomplete remediation
Maple Bay return to Onondaga Nation is recurring policy ask in letter; may signal sustained constituent/tribal pressure
Contact: Betty Hill, American Indian Law Alliance, Liverpool
Tag warning: Unknown tag 'environmental-remediation' not in taxonomy
